prevalence.value: 8.4% in children under 5 for acute respiratory infections (including pneumonia)
prevalence.trend: Stable but with high seasonal variation due to monsoons

incidence.value: Approximately 20-30 cases per 1,000 population annually, with higher rates (up to 50 per 1,000) in children under 5
incidence.source: WHO Global Health Observatory and ICMR estimates

burden.dalyPerCapita: 0.018 DALYs per capita (high burden from lower respiratory infections)
burden.mortalityRate: 25-30 deaths per 100,000 population, with ~1 million child deaths globally, significant share in India
burden.source: Global Burden of Disease Study 2019 (IHME), extrapolated

riskFactors: Indoor and outdoor air pollution (major contributor in urban and rural India)
vulnerablePopulations: Children under 5 years (high burden, ~40% of cases)
Low socioeconomic groups in rural and urban slums
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: WHO criteria: Cough or difficult breathing with fast breathing or chest indrawing; confirmed by chest X-ray (consolidation) and microbiological tests (sputum culture, blood tests)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Low (20-30% for childhood ARI via ASHA workers and ICDS programs)
publicHealthSystem.estimatedPatients: Over 50 million cases annually, with ~2-3 million in children under 5
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Moderate (50-60% effective treatment coverage, limited by AMR)

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Adapted WHO guidelines with emphasis on clinical assessment in resource-limited settings
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Antibiotics (amoxicillin as first-line per NTEP/WHO), oxygen therapy, and PCV13 vaccination; integrated with RNTCP for TB co-infection
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High antimicrobial resistance, air pollution exacerbating urban incidence (e.g., Delhi NCR), poor rural access, and overcrowding in monsoon seasons
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: National vaccination drive (PCV introduced 2017, coverage ~70% in children), robust ICMR surveillance, and community health worker programs
